title: Canadian Gender Budgeting Act
description: Canadian Gender Budgeting Act (Canadian Gender Budgeting Act). Current to 2026-02-04.

provisions.title: Short title
provisions.content: 1 This Act may be cited as the Canadian Gender Budgeting Act .

provisions.title: Policy statement
provisions.content: 2 It is declared to be the policy of the Government of Canada to (a) promote the principle of gender equality and greater inclusiveness in society as part of the annual federal budget, in support of Canada’s long-term economic growth and prosperity; (b) consider gender and diversity in taxation and resource allocation decisions, including in respect of direct spending and transfers to persons and other levels of government; (c) make information available to the public on the impacts of Government decisions in terms of gender and diversity, in order to enhance transparency and accountability; and (d) strengthen the ongoing capacity of departments named in Schedule VI to the Financial Administration Act to consider gender and diversity in the development of policy in a budgetary context, including through guidance, best practices and expertise provided by the Minister for Women and Gender Equality.

provisions.title: Report — new budget measures
provisions.content: 3 The Minister of Finance must table, before each House of Parliament, on any of the first 30 days on which that House is sitting after the day on which a budget plan is tabled in Parliament, a report on the impacts in terms of gender and diversity of all new budget measures described in the plan, if an assessment of the impacts is not included in the budget plan or any related documents that the Minister has made public.

provisions.title: Analysis — tax expenditures
provisions.content: 4 Once a year, the Minister of Finance must make available to the public analysis of impacts in terms of gender and diversity of the tax expenditures, such as tax exemptions, deductions or credits, that the Minister considers appropriate.

provisions.title: Analysis — programs
provisions.content: 5 Once a year, the President of the Treasury Board must make available to the public analysis of impacts in terms of gender and diversity of the existing Government of Canada expenditure programs that the President, in consultation with the Minister of Finance, considers appropriate.
